prose:
Fukui Reactivity Indices for Pyridine

**Fukui reactivity indices** (f+, f-, f0, dual descriptor) for pyridine were computed using **DFT** at the **tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP**, density-fit) level of theory with PySCF, in the **gas phase**, with a **charge of 0** and **multiplicity of 1**. The geometry was built from the user-provided `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz`. This was a single-point calculation. The SCF converged successfully. The sum of f+ and f- indices passed the charge conservation checks.

The live `.out` log for this calculation is available at `fukui_20260704-191221.out`.

**Results:**

The most electrophilic atom is Atom 1 (Nitrogen), with a Fukui f+ index of 0.48423.
The most nucleophilic atom is Atom 4 (Carbon), with a Fukui f- index of 0.11826.

| Atom | Symbol | f+       | f-       | f0       | Dual Descriptor |
|:-----|:-------|:---------|:---------|:---------|:----------------|
| 1    | N      | 0.48423  | 0.06412  | 0.27417  | 0.42010         |
| 2    | C      | 0.32447  | 0.00619  | 0.16533  | 0.31828         |
| 3    | C      | 0.14032  | 0.11823  | 0.12928  | 0.02209         |
| 4    | C      | 0.14040  | 0.11826  | 0.12933  | 0.02213         |
| 5    | C      | 0.29963  | 0.11207  | 0.20585  | 0.18756         |
| 6    | C      | 0.29948  | 0.11201  | 0.20574  | 0.18747         |
| 7    | H      | -0.14151 | 0.08285  | -0.02933 | -0.22436        |
| 8    | H      | -0.13368 | 0.09818  | -0.01775 | -0.23186        |\
| 9    | H      | -0.13366 | 0.09818  | -0.01774 | -0.23184        |
| 10   | H      | -0.13982 | 0.09496  | -0.02243 | -0.23478        |
| 11   | H      | -0.13985 | 0.09495  | -0.02245 | -0.23479        |
